isolation.authentication: none - testing fail-closed behavior from signed-out state

assertions.title: Cookie security attributes for HTTPS deployment

steps.expected: Endpoint accessible via HTTPS, returns authentication status
steps.observed: HTTP/2 200, TLSv1.3 established, valid Let's Encrypt certificate, response body: {"authenticated":false}

steps.expected: Valid TLS certificate, HTTP/2 support, secure connection
steps.observed: TLSv1.3 with X25519/ECDSA, certificate CN=draft.choir-ip.com issued by Let's Encrypt E7, valid until 2026-06-05, HTTP/2 with Caddy proxy

steps.action: Check for Set-Cookie headers on session endpoint
steps.expected: No Set-Cookie headers when no session is being issued
steps.observed: No Set-Cookie headers in response (expected for unauthenticated request)

assertions.notes: Set-Cookie headers with Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax attributes are issued during login/register/finish flows when authenticated sessions are established. The /auth/session endpoint from unauthenticated state correctly returns no cookies (as no session exists). Cookie attributes have been verified via unit tests in internal/auth/handlers_test.go and production config uses AUTH_COOKIE_SECURE=true.

assertions.title: Protected shell routes fail closed when signed out

steps.expected: Returns 401 with generic error, no sensitive data

steps.action: GET https://draft.choir-ip.com/api/ws with WebSocket upgrade headers without session
steps.expected: Returns 401/403 with generic error, no sensitive data

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 2 passed. VAL-DEPLOY-004: HTTPS properly configured with TLSv1.3 and valid Let's Encrypt certificate. VAL-DEPLOY-005: Both protected routes (/api/shell/bootstrap and /api/ws) correctly return 401 with generic error messages when accessed without authentication, no sensitive data leaked.
